context: A Spanish bar or cafe is where half of daily life happens. You can often stand right at the bar, order in a few words, and pay when you leave - no need to wait to be seated or to flag anyone down. It is also the easiest place to duck in for the toilet.
explanation: Ordering is just "quiero" (I would like) plus what you want, finished with "por favor": "Un cafe, por favor". You don't have to build a full sentence - the drink alone, said politely, is enough. The same trick covers a need: "necesito" (I need) plus a thing, as in "Necesito un baño".

examples.es: Un cafe con leche, por favor.

examples.note: Ask for it when you're ready - it rarely comes on its own.
"Servicios" is the word on the door; "el baño" works too.
